text.title: integration-testing Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for integration-testing as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Testing against a production database or live external API in CI.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Network failures and data mutations cause flaky tests and real side effects. Use Testcontainers for databases and respx/WireMock for HTTP dependencies.

failure-mode.description: Mocking the integration layer to test integration behavior.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: If you mock the database in an "integration test", you are writing a unit test. Integration tests must use real (or containerized) services to validate the actual connection.

failure-mode.description: Sharing mutable test data across integration tests without rollback or cleanup.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Tests become order-dependent. One test's inserted row can cause another test's unique constraint violation, creating intermittent failures that are hard to reproduce.
